title: Public task, invalid types of C++ function parameters
body:
version:
Verilator 4.218 2022-01-17 rev v4.216-92-ge6554e0

Please find attached a test in test_regress format.
[v4.218_task.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/8115690/v4.218_task.zip)

When following Verilog code is converted into C++, the generated C++ code is invalid and fails on compilation

```
task test_task(input [19:0] in [2], output [19:0] out [2]);
/*verilator public*/
begin
  out[0] = in[1];
  out[1] = in[0];
end
endtask
```

Generated C++ code:
```
void Vt_task_Test::test_task(uint32_t in, uint32_t& out) {
    VL_DEBUG_IF(VL_DBG_MSGF("+        Vt_task_Test::test_task\n"); );
    // Body
    out[0U] = in[1U];
    out[1U] = in[0U];
}
```

C++ compilation fails with:
```
Vt_task_Test__DepSet_ha3be7d01__0.cpp: In member function 'void Vt_task_Test::test_task(uint32_t, uint32_t&)':
Vt_task_Test__DepSet_ha3be7d01__0.cpp:13:11: error: invalid types 'uint32_t {aka unsigned int}[unsigned int]' for array subscript
   13 |     out[0U] = in[1U];
      |           ^
```

Correct code would be:
```
void Vt_task_Test::test_task(const uint32_t (& in)[2], uint32_t (& out)[2])
...
```

Thanks,
Pawel
